description: Implement Tier 3 features: Multi-model disagreement as volatility signal, Kelly criterion position sizing, Monte Carlo risk simulation. Also create the v3 MQL5 EA (Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5) that writes tick data + spread CSV files for Python to read.
steps: Create python_bridge_v3/strategies/disagreement_signal.py: A DisagreementSignal class. Methods: compute_disagreement(individual_predictions: dict) -> float (0.0=total agreement, 1.0=maximum disagreement); predict_volatility_spike(disagreement: float, current_atr: float) -> (spike_probability: float, expected_atr_multiplier: float); get_position_adjustment(disagreement: float) -> float (multiplier for lot size: 1.0 at low disagreement, 0.5 at high disagreement); get_timing_signal(disagreement: float) -> str ('enter_now', 'wait', 'reduce_size'). The class tracks disagreement history and correlates with subsequent realized volatility to calibrate its predictions.

Create python_bridge_v3/strategies/kelly_sizing.py: A KellySizer class implementing the Kelly criterion for optimal position sizing. Methods: compute_kelly_fraction(win_rate: float, avg_win: float, avg_loss: float) -> float (the full Kelly fraction); get_optimal_lot(account_balance: float, risk_per_trade: float, win_rate: float, avg_win: float, avg_loss: float, current_atr: float) -> float (lot size using fractional Kelly with safety factor); is_kelly_valid() -> bool (True only if we have enough trades and win_rate > 50%). Uses Brain's tracked win_rate and avg_win/avg_loss ratio. Apply fractional Kelly (default 0.25x full Kelly) for safety. Clamp to min/max lot from config.

Create python_bridge_v3/strategies/monte_carlo.py: A MonteCarloRiskSimulator class. Methods: simulate_scenarios(current_drawdown: float, win_rate: float, avg_win: float, avg_loss: float, losing_streak: int, regime: str, num_sims: int=1000) -> dict with keys: ruin_probability (probability of hitting daily loss limit), expected_drawdown (median drawdown across simulations), max_drawdown_95pct (95th percentile worst drawdown), safe_to_trade (bool: True if ruin_probability < threshold); should_skip_trade() -> (skip: bool, reason: str) based on latest simulation results. Run simulation before each trade using current account state. If probability of hitting daily loss limit > 5%, skip the trade.

Update python_bridge_v3/strategies/signal_generator.py: (1) After ensemble prediction, compute disagreement = disagreement_signal.compute_disagreement(individual_predictions). If disagreement > strong_threshold, log warning and apply position_adjustment from disagreement_signal. (2) Replace fixed position sizing with kelly_sizer.get_optimal_lot() when enable_kelly_sizing is True and kelly is valid. Fall back to existing sizing if insufficient trade history. (3) Before final signal generation, call monte_carlo.should_skip_trade() - if skip=True, return HOLD. (4) Use disagreement signal to adjust tp_pips when volatility spike is predicted (widen TP on expected high vol).

Update python_bridge_v3/strategies/trading_brain.py: Feed Kelly sizing data to Brain's risk evaluation. Add disagreement as an input to the Brain's trade scoring system. Update Brain's evaluate() to consider Monte Carlo risk output.

Update python_bridge_v3/main.py: Initialize DisagreementSignal, KellySizer, MonteCarloRiskSimulator. Wire into signal generation pipeline and trade confirmation feedback loop (so Kelly/MC get updated after each closed trade).

Create Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5 at the repo root: Copy Python_Bridge_EA_v2.mq5 as base. Make these changes: (a) Update version to '8.00', update all comments to say v3/v8.0. (b) Change all file paths: InpSignalFile default to 'python_bridge_v3_signal.csv', InpConfirmFile to 'python_bridge_v3_confirm.csv', InpExitFile to 'python_bridge_v3_exit.csv', InpHeartbeatFile to 'python_bridge_v3_heartbeat.txt', InpStatusFile to 'python_bridge_v3_status.txt'. (c) Add a new function WriteTickData() that on every tick writes a line to 'python_bridge_v3_tick_data.csv' with format: timestamp,bid,ask,last_volume,tick_flags. Use MqlTick structure with SymbolInfoTick(). Buffer last 5000 ticks in memory, write full buffer periodically (every 100ms via a static timer check). (d) Add WriteSpreadData() function that writes 'python_bridge_v3_spread.csv' with current spread: timestamp,spread_points,ask,bid. Write every tick (small file, overwrites each time). (e) Call WriteTickData() and WriteSpreadData() from OnTick(). (f) Add input bool InpWriteTickData = true to enable/disable tick writing. (g) Update brain settings file path to 'python_bridge_v3_brain_settings.csv'. (h) Update mt5 heartbeat to 'mt5_bridge_v3_heartbeat.txt'. IMPORTANT: Never use 'input' as a variable name in MQL5 code - it is a reserved keyword.

Update python_bridge_v3/signals/bridge.py: Update all default file paths to use v3 prefixes. Add read_tick_data() method that reads the tick data CSV. Add read_spread_data() method that reads the spread CSV. Update MT5_HEARTBEAT_FILE to v3 path.
acceptance_criteria: python_bridge_v3/strategies/kelly_sizing.py exists with KellySizer implementing fractional Kelly
python_bridge_v3/strategies/monte_carlo.py exists with MonteCarloRiskSimulator that gates trades on ruin probability
Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5 exists at repo root with v3 file paths and tick data/spread writing functions
All v3 file paths are consistent between EA and Python (signal, confirm, tick, spread, heartbeat, brain settings)
signal_generator.py integrates disagreement, Kelly, and Monte Carlo with config flag guards
main.py initializes and wires all Tier 3 components
All Python imports work without errors
The MQL5 EA does NOT use 'input' as a variable name anywhere (only as keyword prefix for input parameters)
verification: Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from strategies.disagreement_signal import DisagreementSignal; from strategies.kelly_sizing import KellySizer; from strategies.monte_carlo import MonteCarloRiskSimulator; print('All Tier 3 modules import OK')"

Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from strategies.kelly_sizing import KellySizer; ks = KellySizer(); lot = ks.compute_kelly_fraction(0.6, 2.0, 1.0); print(f'Kelly fraction: {lot:.4f}'); assert 0 < lot < 1, 'Kelly out of range'"

Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from strategies.monte_carlo import MonteCarloRiskSimulator; mc = MonteCarloRiskSimulator(); result = mc.simulate_scenarios(0.02, 0.6, 2.0, 1.0, 0, 'trending'); print(f'Ruin prob: {result[\"ruin_probability\"]:.4f}'); print('Monte Carlo works')"
Run: python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'python_bridge_v3'); from main import PythonMLBridge; print('Full v3 system import OK')"
Run: test -f Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5 && echo 'EA v3 exists'
Run: grep -c 'python_bridge_v3' Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5 to confirm v3 paths are used
Run: grep -c '\binput\b' Python_Bridge_EA_v3.mq5 | head -1 (verify 'input' only used as MQL5 keyword for parameter declarations, never as variable name)
